items.summary: South Australian police have concluded their latest search for evidence following the disappearance of four-year-old Gus Lamont, but have not ruled out returning to the remote station where he lived.

items.title: Novak Djokovic loses to Jack Draper in three sets at Indian Wells tournament

items.title: Queanbeyan's main street will be closed to cars for six months. Here's what you need to know

items.summary: This week in Canberra the Nationals chose a new leader, the energy minister chose his words carefully and the government chose to avoid one word altogether.

items.title: Wife of Australian footballer Barry Cable takes stand in his child sex abuse trial

items.title: Tigers hopeful Heamasi Makasini stays with NRL club amid talk of rugby union switch

items.title: Fuel standards relaxed to add 100 million litres of fuel to Australian supply
items.summary: Australia will bring in 100 million more litres of petrol by allowing a higher level of sulfur content in its fuel for the next 60 days, Energy Minister Chris Bowen says, as the country reckons with petrol flows choked by the Middle East war.

items.summary: The oil price has surged again amid Iranian strikes on oil tankers and the fear there could be more attacks to come.

items.summary: The boil water alert for Darwin has been lifted after testing confirmed the region's water was free of E.coli and other harmful bacteria.

items.summary: A West Australian man honoured with a prestigious award for excellence in child protection has been charged with raping a teenage boy.

items.summary: Here's how you can become a more responsible dog owner — according to your fellow readers.

items.title: Deeper oil crisis could add full percentage point to inflation, Treasury warns
items.summary: Treasury has advised the government that if the oil price averages $US100 per barrel for three months, there would be a temporary 0.5 percentage point spike to headline inflation.

items.title: Atlassian cuts 1,600 jobs from global workforce as AI slashes labour needs
items.summary: Atlassian's Australian workforce will account for 30 per cent of the tech giant's global job cuts, with the freed-up funds to be invested in AI and enterprise sales.

items.title: Beth Mooney sold for record fee in Women's Hundred auction

items.title: Man found wounded at supermarket assisting police as woman and child found dead
items.summary: After speaking with a wounded man at a supermarket in Logan about 7:30 this morning, police attended a nearby address and found the bodies of a woman and child.

items.title: Islamic Society criticises 'competition' to attack Muslims as SA Liberals stand by candidate
items.summary: An organisation representing South Australian Muslims criticises the Liberal Party and One Nation for what it says is a rush to attack Islam "more harshly in order to gain political support" ahead of the state election.

items.summary: The pair were reported missing when they failed to reach their destination while driving north on Tuesday, with police confirming two bodies were found inside their car.

items.title: Cambodia says it will shut down online scam centres by the end of April

items.title: Former ASIO boss Dennis Richardson says he was 'surplus to requirements' of royal commission

items.title: Australia's summers are getting longer with more extreme heat
items.summary: Australia has always been hot. On average, summer is now one degree warmer than it was 50 years ago. But that number hides the stark reality that the summers our kids experience today are much more extreme and longer than they used to be.

items.title: Jason Bennett returns to VFL coverage with the ABC after television and radio deal struck
items.summary: Respected VFL commentator Jason Bennett returns to the coverage in 2026 after the ABC picked up the broadcast rights to the league.

items.title: Iran in doubt to play men's FIFA World Cup following US air strikes

items.title: Why IV drips are surging in popularity in Australia's wellness space
items.summary: Some advocates say vitamin IV drips can promote clearer thinking, immunity, beauty and even anti-aging, but health experts say there is little research to back the claims.

items.title: Iconic Australian-made pinball machines preserved for generations to come
items.summary: Five pinball machines, including the first licensed Star Wars unit, have been saved from the scrap heap and will eventually be made available for the public to play at a special exhibition.

items.title: Record 400 million barrels of oil to be released from reserves to stall price hike
items.summary: The International Energy Agency agrees to release 400 million barrels of oil, the largest such move in its history. Australia says it is considering the decision, which would not involve sending oil overseas.

items.title: Approval granted for major expansion of Porters Bridge Road Quarry despite concerns
items.summary: Locals say the "continuous rumbling" from a quarry in Tasmania's Meander Valley is a major disturbance. The company which owns it has just been given approval to increase production by 500 per cent.

items.title: Victorian wineries sell vineyards to gold exploration company amid global headwinds
items.summary: As the wine industry struggles with increased challenges, some Victorian wineries are choosing to sell their land to mining companies.

items.title: The real-life story behind The Voice of Hind Rajab

items.title: Victorian government vows to shift building inspection costs from buyers to sellers

items.title: Matt Canavan may blunt One Nation but can he help rebuild the Coalition?

items.title: Former spy boss Dennis Richardson resigns from antisemitism royal commission

items.title: Bird deaths linked to fish lost in algal bloom, SA Research and Development Institute says
items.summary: Birds are starving and dying along metropolitan Adelaide beaches, caused by a lack of fish as a result of the algal bloom, the South Australian government says.

items.title: WA government rules out petrol price cap as fuel costs soar amid Middle East war

items.summary: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

items.title: Flower importers feel the pinch as Iran war affects flight routes
